What is the hymenium type of this plant?
answer: The hymenium is the spore-producing tissue layer of the fruit body, consisting of hyphae that extend into the gills and terminate as end cells. Various cell types in the hymenium have microscopic characteristics used to identify or distinguish species. The spore-bearing cells, known as basidia, are four-spored and measure 37–45 μm long by 8–10 μm wide at the thickest point.
